# Codebase Review Ledger

This ledger tracks systematic review work across `lotus-core`.

Companion process document:

- [CODEBASE-REVIEW-PLAYBOOK.md](./CODEBASE-REVIEW-PLAYBOOK.md)

| CR-1373 | 2026-07-05 | Degraded source-data contract tests | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #609 was valid: degraded, stale, fallback, partial, unavailable, and blocking source-data states were covered unevenly across product-family tests, leaving no representative contract suite that proved degraded Core evidence could not masquerade as fresh authoritative data. | Added `test_degraded_source_data_contracts.py` across positions, transactions, market data, instrument reference, valuation, cashflow, and reconciliation/control responses; asserted data-quality, freshness, supportability, source-proof/fallback details, and correlation semantics; and added a guarded HoldingsAsOf degraded API example. | No runtime behavior, route path, response field removal, persistence schema, Kafka contract, or topology changed. Dependency timeout/hard-failure examples remain covered by existing ingestion problem-response tests and catalog entries; future source-data dependency clients should add focused degraded/problem tests when introduced. #609 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1373-DEGRADED-SOURCE-DATA-CONTRACT-TESTS.md](./CR-1373-DEGRADED-SOURCE-DATA-CONTRACT-TESTS.md); focused degraded-source contract tests, API example guard, docs/architecture gates, scoped lint/format, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1372 | 2026-07-05 | Holdings degradation source metadata | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #601 was valid: HoldingsAsOf could merge snapshot rows, history rows, and fallback valuation evidence while exposing only aggregate `data_quality_status`, leaving downstream consumers to infer fallback provenance and freshness. | Added reusable source-data degradation DTOs, isolated HoldingsAsOf degradation policy in `position_holdings_degradation.py`, exposed row/field-scoped fallback, stale, partial, unavailable, and empty-state details, and added deterministic HoldingsAsOf content hash/source digest/source refs/source lineage. | This is an additive response-contract change. Existing holdings behavior, route paths, request parameters, persistence, and runtime topology are preserved. Same-pattern scan found cash-balance fallback as related future scope; #601 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1372-HOLDINGS-DEGRADATION-SOURCE-METADATA.md](./CR-1372-HOLDINGS-DEGRADATION-SOURCE-METADATA.md); focused holdings/source-metadata/OpenAPI tests, scoped Ruff, API/docs/architecture gates, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1369 | 2026-07-05 | Load-run progress query round trips | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #577 was valid: `OperationsRepository.get_load_run_progress(...)` executed 14 scalar statements and 4 row statements sequentially for one operator summary response. | Added a composed scalar-row statement that returns the same 14 scalar facts in one DB execute, preserved the four existing aggregate row queries, and updated repository tests to prove zero `db.scalar(...)` calls, five total executes, SQL shape, and unchanged response fields. | Response contract, OpenAPI schema, database schema, and runtime topology are unchanged. Further consolidation of the four remaining row queries needs separate plan evidence; #577 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1369-LOAD-RUN-PROGRESS-QUERY-ROUND-TRIPS.md](./CR-1369-LOAD-RUN-PROGRESS-QUERY-ROUND-TRIPS.md); focused repository tests, scoped Ruff, docs/architecture gates, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1368 | 2026-07-05 | Valuation backfill chunked staging | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #579 was valid: valuation backfill staging still generated jobs per state and called `upsert_jobs(...)` once per state, creating avoidable DB round trips during large backfills. | Added `VALUATION_SCHEDULER_BACKFILL_UPSERT_CHUNK_SIZE`, replaced per-state staging with a bounded cross-state job chunk planner, preserved missing-history normalization/gap metrics/correlation lineage, and added multi-state chunk-boundary tests. | Existing repository idempotency, stale-epoch filtering, Kafka contracts, and runtime topology are preserved. Same-pattern scan found no remaining scheduler per-state `upsert_jobs(...)` loop; #579 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1368-VALUATION-BACKFILL-CHUNKED-STAGING.md](./CR-1368-VALUATION-BACKFILL-CHUNKED-STAGING.md); focused scheduler/settings tests, scoped Ruff, docs/architecture gates, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1367 | 2026-07-05 | Valuation scheduler work budgets | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #578 was valid: the valuation scheduler had batch size and dispatch rounds but no explicit poll-duration budget, dispatch-duration budget, or scheduler-level producer back-pressure signal. Large backfills or slow Kafka delivery could therefore stretch a poll beyond the intended cadence. | Added strict-validation-aware poll and dispatch budget settings, scheduler poll-duration/claimed/dispatched/budget-exhausted/back-pressure metrics, typed valuation publish errors, dispatch-budget stop and recovery behavior, and focused normal/budget/back-pressure tests. | Intentional behavior change: scheduler dispatch stops when poll or dispatch budgets are exhausted, confirms already queued work, and recovers remaining claimed jobs through the existing durable recovery path. Same-pattern scan found aggregation scheduler as adjacent future scope; #578 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1367-VALUATION-SCHEDULER-WORK-BUDGETS.md](./CR-1367-VALUATION-SCHEDULER-WORK-BUDGETS.md); focused scheduler/settings/publisher tests, scoped Ruff, metric vocabulary guard, docs gate, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1366 | 2026-07-05 | Shared retry policy profiles | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #574 was valid: Kafka admin verification and several DB-backed consumers still used fixed waits and local retry counts, creating synchronized retry waves and inconsistent retry taxonomy. | Added `portfolio_common.retry_policy` with bounded exponential jitter profiles, max attempts, max elapsed budgets, explicit retryable exception classes, retry-attempt logs, and `retry_policy_events_total`; migrated Kafka admin, valuation readiness, price-event, cashflow, position, and reconciliation-request consumers; added direct retry-policy tests and ran the migrated consumer suites. | Intentional behavior change: unexpected exceptions are no longer retried by the migrated decorators unless listed as retryable. This preserves existing handling paths for validation/configuration/unexpected errors while reducing retry amplification. #574 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1366-SHARED-RETRY-POLICY-PROFILES.md](./CR-1366-SHARED-RETRY-POLICY-PROFILES.md); focused retry/Kafka admin/consumer tests, scoped Ruff, metric vocabulary guard, docs gate, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1365 | 2026-07-05 | Kafka producer back-pressure telemetry | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #573 was valid as an observability gap: the event publisher port already mapped `BufferError` to retryable `KafkaPublishBackPressure` and flush timeouts to uncertain delivery, but the low-level producer still logged queue saturation as a generic publish error and emitted no bounded producer saturation metric. | Added shared `kafka_producer_events_total` telemetry, emitted accepted/back-pressure/failed outcomes from `KafkaProducer.publish_message`, logged queue-full as `kafka.producer.back_pressure` with reason `queue_full`, added producer-level queue-full and generic failure tests, added a flush-exception uncertain-delivery regression, and documented the producer metric/back-pressure contract. | Existing caller behavior is preserved: `BufferError` still propagates to `KafkaEventPublisher`, which returns retryable back-pressure, and scheduler/outbox recovery paths continue to avoid marking unconfirmed work as dispatched. #573 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1365-KAFKA-PRODUCER-BACK-PRESSURE.md](./CR-1365-KAFKA-PRODUCER-BACK-PRESSURE.md); focused producer/publisher tests, scoped Ruff, metric vocabulary guard, docs gate, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1364 | 2026-07-05 | Kafka producer runtime policy | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #572 was valid: `KafkaProducer` still embedded one hard-coded client id, retry count, batching profile, compression type, delivery timeout, request timeout, and no explicit queue bounds for every producer path. | Added `portfolio_common.kafka_producer_policy.KafkaProducerPolicy`, routed `KafkaProducer` through the shared policy, exposed strict-validation-aware default env settings plus service override JSON, made per-service producer singletons service-name aware, kept idempotence, `acks=all`, and safe in-flight request limits adapter-owned, and added focused producer policy/adapter tests. | Existing default producer reliability and throughput posture remains compatible: default client id, retries, linger, batch size, compression, delivery timeout, and request timeout match the prior hard-coded values; queue bounds now use explicit librdkafka-compatible defaults. Future producer tuning should use the shared policy instead of local dictionaries. #572 locally fixed pending final gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1364-KAFKA-PRODUCER-RUNTIME-POLICY.md](./CR-1364-KAFKA-PRODUCER-RUNTIME-POLICY.md); focused producer tests, scoped Ruff, docs gate, and diff evidence are recorded before commit. |
| CR-1363 | 2026-07-05 | Downstream dependency access policy | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #581 was valid: shared health and Kafka admin helpers still embedded fixed Kafka metadata timeouts, and there was no reusable downstream access policy for future source-data clients to inherit timeout, retry, pagination, batching, cache, and circuit-breaker posture. | Added `portfolio_common.downstream_access.DownstreamAccessPolicy`, wired health readiness and Kafka admin topic verification through it, made timeout/retry/cache/batch/page/circuit settings strict-validation aware, added focused policy/health/Kafka tests, and documented the pattern in repo context and the operations runbook. | Existing default timeout and retry behavior remains compatible: request timeout defaults to 5 seconds, Kafka topic verification defaults to 15 attempts with 4 second backoff and 60 second elapsed budget. Future downstream adapters should consume the shared policy rather than adding local literals. #581 locally fixed pending final lint/docs gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1363-DOWNSTREAM-DEPENDENCY-ACCESS-POLICY.md](./CR-1363-DOWNSTREAM-DEPENDENCY-ACCESS-POLICY.md); focused policy/health/Kafka tests pending final gate run. |
| CR-1362 | 2026-07-05 | Upload parser resource budgets | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #589 was valid beyond the existing byte-stream guard: upload preview/commit still lacked parser-level row, column, and cell-length budgets; XLSX parsing still materialized the worksheet; preview parsing had no route-level rate/abuse control; and content-type/extension mismatches were not rejected before parser work. | Added configurable parser budgets, streamed CSV/XLSX row parsing through budget checks, closed XLSX workbooks after streaming, rejected content-type/extension mismatches, enforced preview and commit rate checks before body parsing, kept content-length and streaming byte guards, and added focused settings, validator, route, docs, wiki, and boundary-standard coverage. | Normal small CSV/XLSX uploads remain compatible. Oversized, over-wide, over-long-cell, mismatched-media, and rate-limited uploads now fail intentionally with 400/413/429. #589 locally fixed pending PR CI/QA and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1362-UPLOAD-PARSER-RESOURCE-BUDGETS.md](./CR-1362-UPLOAD-PARSER-RESOURCE-BUDGETS.md); focused upload/settings/OpenAPI tests, scoped Ruff, wiki/docs gate, security-control gate, import proof, and diff check passed before commit. |
| CR-1361 | 2026-07-05 | Upload preview source-safe samples | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #590 was valid: upload preview returned normalized valid source rows by default, exposing portfolio, client-adjacent, instrument, transaction, quantity, price, fee, tax, and amount fields to callers that only needed validation feedback. | Defaulted preview sample rows to `[]`, added `include_sample_rows`, gated sample rows with signed `ingestion.uploads.preview_samples.read`, redacted sensitive identifiers and monetary/fee/quantity fields, added allow/deny audit evidence, and updated OpenAPI plus focused upload service/router/integration tests. | Response field `sample_rows` remains present for compatibility but is empty by default. Privileged callers must opt in and present signed capability context. #590 locally fixed pending final lint/docs gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1361-UPLOAD-PREVIEW-SOURCE-SAFE-SAMPLES.md](./CR-1361-UPLOAD-PREVIEW-SOURCE-SAFE-SAMPLES.md); focused upload tests passed before final gates. |
| CR-1360 | 2026-07-05 | Enterprise service principal auth context | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #583 was valid: enterprise authorization trusted caller-supplied `X-Service-Identity` and `X-Capabilities`, and treated `Authorization` as a presence marker rather than a verified principal. | Added a signed enterprise auth-context contract with key id, timestamp, HMAC signature, actor/tenant/role/correlation/service/capability binding, freshness validation, settings support, runtime config issue reporting, unsupported Authorization denial, and forged capability/service identity regressions across shared, query, control-plane, and ingestion tests. | Enterprise authorization disabled paths remain unchanged. When read/write authz is enabled, callers must provide signed auth context from a trusted gateway/mTLS boundary. #583 locally fixed pending final docs/security gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1360-ENTERPRISE-SERVICE-PRINCIPAL-AUTH-CONTEXT.md](./CR-1360-ENTERPRISE-SERVICE-PRINCIPAL-AUTH-CONTEXT.md); focused shared/query/control-plane/ingestion enterprise tests and scoped Ruff/format passed before final gates. |
| CR-1359 | 2026-07-05 | Ingestion ops JWT claim policy | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #588 was valid: privileged ingestion ops bearer JWTs were verified with an under-specified HS256 policy where issuer, audience, expiry, issued-at, replay id, principal identity, scope/capability, and key id were not all mandatory, and non-local profiles could keep static token fallback without explicit approval. | Required `exp`, `iat`, `iss`, `aud`, `jti`, one principal identity claim, required ops scope/capability, `kid`, active/previous HS256 key lookup, strict non-local JWT settings, explicit static-token fallback approval, and focused malformed/bad-alg/missing-claim/expired/wrong-issuer/wrong-audience/missing-scope/valid/rotated-key tests. | Local/dev/test startup remains compatible. Non-local and strict profiles now intentionally fail unless JWT issuer, audience, active key id, secret, and required scope are configured; static token fallback requires explicit approval and a non-default token. #588 locally fixed pending final lint/docs gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1359-INGESTION-OPS-JWT-CLAIM-POLICY.md](./CR-1359-INGESTION-OPS-JWT-CLAIM-POLICY.md); focused ops-control/settings/router tests passed before final lint/docs gates. |
| CR-1358 | 2026-07-05 | Query-service page-token key rotation policy | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #587 was valid: query-service cursor/page tokens used a static local default secret in settings and unversioned payload/signature envelopes, with a separate analytics HMAC helper duplicating the same weak pattern. | Added versioned `PageTokenCodec` envelopes with `kid`, expiry, issuer/audience, optional route/tenant binding, active/previous key rotation support, and strict non-local/default-secret/key-id rejection; routed analytics page tokens through the shared codec; added focused codec, settings, analytics, integration, and analytics-timeseries tests. | Local/dev/test defaults remain compatible. Non-local and strict profiles now intentionally fail startup if `LOTUS_CORE_PAGE_TOKEN_SECRET` or `LOTUS_CORE_PAGE_TOKEN_KEY_ID` are missing/default. New tokens use the hardened envelope while decoded payload shape remains unchanged for callers. #587 locally fixed pending final lint/docs gates, PR CI/QA, and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1358-PAGE-TOKEN-KEY-ROTATION-POLICY.md](./CR-1358-PAGE-TOKEN-KEY-ROTATION-POLICY.md); focused token/settings and caller-heavy service tests passed before final lint/docs gates. |
| CR-1357 | 2026-07-05 | Ingestion enterprise write authorization | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #582 was valid: `ingestion_service` was classified as an enterprise-middleware business API but lacked service-owned default capability rules for canonical ingestion write routes, and operational probes needed an explicit unauthenticated allowlist under read authorization. | Added an ingestion service-local enterprise-readiness wrapper, default capability rules for ingestion write families, shared service-owned default rule merging, explicit operational-path allowlisting, and focused route-coverage, authorization, denied-audit, and app middleware tests. | Existing local/dev/test behavior remains opt-in. In production-like or explicitly enforced environments, ingestion writes now intentionally require actor, tenant, role, correlation id, service identity, and route-specific write capability. No route path, DTO, OpenAPI schema, Kafka contract, database schema, upload budget behavior, or runtime topology changed. #582 locally fixed pending PR CI/QA and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1357-INGESTION-ENTERPRISE-WRITE-AUTHORIZATION.md](./CR-1357-INGESTION-ENTERPRISE-WRITE-AUTHORIZATION.md); focused enterprise-readiness and ingestion app tests passed before final lint/docs gates. |
| CR-1356 | 2026-07-05 | Capability rule exact path matching | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #584 was valid: templated enterprise capability rules matched prefixes, so `GET /portfolios/{portfolio_id}` could authorize deeper child routes such as `/portfolios/P1/analytics` without explicit subtree intent. | Made literal and templated capability rules exact by default; added explicit `/**` subtree syntax; updated tests and examples that intentionally authorize child routes; added regressions for exact template match, deeper child denial, wildcard subtree allow, and specificity ordering. | Intentional security behavior change: child routes now require exact rules or explicit `/**` subtree rules. Generated source-data capability rules already use concrete route templates and remain exact. No API route path, OpenAPI schema, database schema, Kafka contract, metric name, Dockerfile, or runtime topology changed. #584 locally fixed pending PR CI/QA and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1356-CAPABILITY-RULE-EXACT-PATH-MATCHING.md](./CR-1356-CAPABILITY-RULE-EXACT-PATH-MATCHING.md); focused enterprise readiness/settings tests and scoped Ruff/format checks passed before commit. |

Strict or non-local deployments now intentionally fail fast on invalid shared runtime settings. #600 can move to fixed-local after this commit and focused validation, pending PR CI/QA and merge. | Evidence: [CR-1355-PORTFOLIO-COMMON-CONFIG-RUNTIME-STRICTNESS.md](./CR-1355-PORTFOLIO-COMMON-CONFIG-RUNTIME-STRICTNESS.md); focused config/runtime/Kafka consumer tests, scoped Ruff, and format checks passed before commit. |
| CR-1354 | 2026-07-05 | Portfolio aggregation runtime settings strictness | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #600 remained valid for portfolio aggregation because `portfolio_aggregation_service.app.settings` still parsed consumer count, scheduler poll interval, batch size, stale timeout, and max attempts with a local silent-fallback helper. Invalid non-local resilience controls could be hidden until runtime load. | Replaced the local positive-int parser with `portfolio_common.runtime_settings.env_int`; preserved local clamp-to-one behavior through `minimum_fallback=1`; added strict production malformed-value, explicit strict non-positive, and local fallback logging tests. | Existing unset defaults, setting names, dataclass fields, local fallback behavior, scheduler behavior, consumer manager behavior, route paths, OpenAPI metadata, database schema, Kafka contracts, metric names, Dockerfiles, and runtime topology are unchanged. Strict or non-local deployments now intentionally fail startup on invalid portfolio aggregation runtime settings. Remaining shared-config fallback paths were handled by CR-1355. | Evidence: [CR-1354-PORTFOLIO-AGGREGATION-RUNTIME-SETTINGS-STRICTNESS.md](./CR-1354-PORTFOLIO-AGGREGATION-RUNTIME-SETTINGS-STRICTNESS.md); focused aggregation runtime tests, shared runtime settings tests, scoped Ruff, and format checks passed before commit. |
| CR-1353 | 2026-07-05 | Analytics lineage metadata collision guard | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #705 showed Core analytics reference and portfolio-timeseries live proof calls could fail before downstream performance/risk proof generation completed when response construction received duplicate `lineage` values from response-level lineage and runtime source metadata. The same pattern can affect adjacent analytics-input response assembly if source runtime metadata drifts back from `source_lineage` to `lineage`. | Added a guarded analytics runtime metadata helper; routed portfolio timeseries, position timeseries, and portfolio reference responses through it; added canonical `PB_SG_GLOBAL_BAL_001` regression tests; added a collision test proving future duplicate-lineage metadata fails through `AnalyticsInputError` instead of unhandled constructor `TypeError`; updated repo context. | Existing successful response shape, route path, request DTO, response DTO, OpenAPI schema, database schema, Kafka contract, metric name, Dockerfile, deployment topology, and runtime service boundary are unchanged. Direct live local Core probes for portfolio timeseries and analytics reference now return `200 OK` with a single response `lineage`, `content_hash`, and matching `source_digest`; portfolio timeseries still reports `freshness_status=STALE`, so downstream performance/risk/idea proof reruns remain pending. This locally fixes the direct Core 500s in #705 pending PR CI/QA, downstream proof validation, merge, and issue closure. | Evidence: [CR-1353-ANALYTICS-LINEAGE-METADATA-COLLISION-GUARD.md](./CR-1353-ANALYTICS-LINEAGE-METADATA-COLLISION-GUARD.md); focused analytics service tests, domain-product metadata tests, source-data identity tests, scoped Ruff, format checks, wiki/docs gate, and live Core endpoint probes passed before commit. |

| CR-1295 | 2026-07-04 | Ingestion job lifecycle fencing | Hardened locally | GitHub issue #701 was valid: ingestion job lifecycle mutations updated rows by `job_id` only, and replay success used separate retry-count and queued-status mutations that could leave operator state inconsistent under concurrent repair, retry, worker failure, or replay paths. | Added expected-status predicates and boolean update outcomes to queued, failed, and retried lifecycle mutations; added an atomic retry-plus-queued transition; rewired ingestion retry and consumer-DLQ replay success to use that atomic transition; made stale bookkeeping repair return a governed conflict; and added focused tests for accepted-to-queued success, stale queued rejection, expected-status SQL, atomic retry+queued behavior, replay conflict responses, and repair conflict responses. | Normal successful ingestion, retry, replay, failure recording, audit, and repair behavior is unchanged when the job is in the expected state. Race cases now produce explicit conflict outcomes instead of silent last-writer-wins updates. Repo context and Ingestion/Event Replay wiki source were updated with the guarded lifecycle rule. Broader follow-up can update direct ingestion routers to surface false lifecycle outcomes where client-visible conflict handling is useful. | Evidence: [CR-1295-INGESTION-JOB-LIFECYCLE-FENCING.md](./CR-1295-INGESTION-JOB-LIFECYCLE-FENCING.md); focused lifecycle/replay/repair tests passed with 33 tests, scoped Ruff lint/format passed, wiki docs gate and strict wiki quality audit passed, wiki check-only reported expected pre-merge publication drift, and `git diff --check` passed with CRLF normalization warnings only. |

Valid correlated DLQ events no longer become falsely `not_replayable` because their ingestion job fell outside a generic list page. Repo context and Event Replay wiki source were updated with the durable no-list-scan recovery rule. No route path, health schema, product API, database schema, or Kafka contract changed. | Evidence: [CR-1249-HEALTH-DEPENDENCY-METRICS.md](./CR-1249-HEALTH-DEPENDENCY-METRICS.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment before commit. |
| CR-1248 | 2026-07-01 | HTTP traceparent span context | Hardened | GitHub issue #492 is valid: standard HTTP bootstrap emitted a W3C-shaped `traceparent` header with a repeated fixed span id, which made correlation-only fallback behavior look like real distributed span context. | Added shared non-zero span-id normalization/generation, rejected all-zero trace/span IDs, removed the hard-coded HTTP fallback span id, preserved inbound valid `traceparent`, derived `traceparent` from `X-Trace-Id` with a fresh span id, and documented the exact W3C propagation boundary without claiming OpenTelemetry export. | Keep future full OpenTelemetry/exporter work separate from this propagation contract. Existing routes, response bodies, event schemas, Kafka topics, and correlation headers are preserved; only the generated HTTP fallback span id changed. | Evidence: [CR-1248-HTTP-TRACEPARENT-SPAN-CONTEXT.md](./CR-1248-HTTP-TRACEPARENT-SPAN-CONTEXT.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment before commit. |
| CR-1247 | 2026-07-01 | Async traceparent propagation | Hardened | GitHub issue #493 is valid: HTTP request handling produced trace context, but the async event path only propagated `correlation_id` through outbox, Kafka, consumer, DLQ, and replay paths. This broke incident traceability across Core's event-driven processing chain. | Added shared traceparent normalization/context helpers; preserved incoming HTTP `traceparent`; extended governed event envelope metadata with `traceparent`; propagated traceparent through outbox payloads, dispatcher Kafka headers, shared consumer context, DLQ payload/headers, direct ingestion publish headers, and DLQ replay headers; added focused unit and integration proof. | Keep future direct Kafka topic catalog/guard work aligned with the new traceparent header contract when direct publish coverage expands. No route, topic, business payload, or existing correlation behavior changed. | Evidence: [CR-1247-ASYNC-TRACEPARENT-PROPAGATION.md](./CR-1247-ASYNC-TRACEPARENT-PROPAGATION.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment before commit. |
| CR-1246 | 2026-07-01 | Outbox recovery integrated proof | Hardened | GitHub issue #670 had implementation coverage across dispatcher failure metadata, QCP diagnostics, governed requeue/audit, recovery audit listing, and metrics, but the remaining local closure gap was integrated proof that one dispatcher-created terminal failure can be diagnosed, requeued, audited, and later processed by the dispatcher. The integration cleanup contract also omitted `outbox_recovery_audit`, risking leaked recovery evidence between DB-backed tests. | Added a Docker-backed integration test for dispatcher terminal failure -> operations failed-row diagnostics -> governed service requeue/audit -> dispatcher success, and added `outbox_recovery_audit` to global test cleanup. | Issue #670 can be marked fixed locally pending PR/CI/QA after focused validation. Alert thresholds for recovery rejection/error metrics remain a runtime-usage follow-up. | Evidence: [CR-1246-OUTBOX-RECOVERY-INTEGRATED-PROOF.md](./CR-1246-OUTBOX-RECOVERY-INTEGRATED-PROOF.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment before commit. |

If route-level retry-state diagnostics are needed later, expose them through protected QCP support views without raw payloads. Existing API routes, OpenAPI contracts, Kafka topics, event payloads, event headers, producer API, and consumers are preserved. | Evidence: [CR-1245-OUTBOX-RETRY-BUDGET-TELEMETRY.md](./CR-1245-OUTBOX-RETRY-BUDGET-TELEMETRY.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment. |
| CR-1244 | 2026-07-01 | Outbox dispatch leased claims | Hardened | GitHub issue #580 is valid: the outbox dispatcher selected pending rows with `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` and then waited on Kafka publish/flush inside the same database transaction, turning broker latency into row-lock duration. | Added nullable `claim_token` and `claim_expires_at` outbox fields plus indexes and migration; split dispatch into short claim transaction, Kafka publish/flush outside row locks, and claim-token-fenced result persistence; added configurable claim lease settings and focused DB-backed tests for committed claims, reclaim, stale-token fencing, success, partial delivery, timeout, and concurrency. | Keep issue #580 open for PR/CI/QA evidence. Preserve the reusable leased-claim/fenced-result pattern for future durable claim-and-publish loops. Existing API routes, OpenAPI contracts, Kafka topics, outbox payloads, event headers, and consumer contracts are preserved; the intentional behavior change is internal lease/fencing of pending outbox dispatch rows. | Evidence: [CR-1244-OUTBOX-DISPATCH-LEASED-CLAIMS.md](./CR-1244-OUTBOX-DISPATCH-LEASED-CLAIMS.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment. |

| CR-1231 | 2026-07-01 | Transaction date semantic contract | Hardened | GitHub issue #440 is valid: the temporal vocabulary and RFC-0083 target model classify current `transaction_date` as the transaction event/trade timestamp and warn not to overload it as `booking_date`, while `TransactionRecord.transaction_date` described itself as a booking timestamp. An adjacent `SellDisposalRecord.transaction_date` description also used booked wording and fed the shared API vocabulary inventory. | Updated the DTO/OpenAPI wording for `TransactionRecord.transaction_date` and `SellDisposalRecord.transaction_date`, corrected adjacent `settlement_date` wording, updated the wealth-reporting API guide, regenerated the API vocabulary inventory, refreshed local OpenAPI artifacts, and added OpenAPI regression assertions that reject booking terminology for transaction-date fields. | Keep issue #440 open for PR/CI/QA evidence. Existing route paths, field names, response shapes, sort/filter behavior, persistence schema, and source-data product contracts are preserved; the intentional change is public documentation/OpenAPI semantic truth only. Future `booking_date` work must introduce an explicit field and migration plan rather than relabeling `transaction_date`. | Evidence: [CR-1231-TRANSACTION-DATE-SEMANTIC-CONTRACT.md](./CR-1231-TRANSACTION-DATE-SEMANTIC-CONTRACT.md); final validation evidence is recorded in the CR and issue comment. |
| CR-1230 | 2026-07-01 | Analytics export execution timeout | Hardened | GitHub issue #597 is valid: analytics export job creation reserved a durable job, marked it running, and then collected/materialized export results synchronously without an explicit current-execution timeout or cancellation-state recovery. Existing stale-job logic only handled reused in-flight rows. | Added configurable `LOTUS_CORE_ANALYTICS_EXPORT_EXECUTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, bounded export collection/completion with `asyncio.wait_for`, extracted `_execute_export_job(...)`, marked observed timeouts and cancellations as durable failed jobs with bounded reason text, preserved cancellation propagation, and added focused tests for timeout, cancellation, settings strictness, stale reuse, fresh reuse, and failure visibility. | Keep issue #597 open for PR/CI/QA evidence and Docker-backed query-service runtime proof. Existing route paths, response DTOs, OpenAPI schemas, database schema, result payloads, and source-data contracts are preserved; the intentional behavior change is durable failed state on observed execution timeout or cancellation. A full async export worker remains a separate future slice if inline execution becomes insufficient. | Evidence: [CR-1230-ANALYTICS-EXPORT-EXECUTION-TIMEOUT.md](./CR-1230-ANALYTICS-EXPORT-EXECUTION-TIMEOUT.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR before commit. |
| CR-1229 | 2026-07-01 | Scheduler dispatch recovery | Hardened | GitHub issue #596 is valid: valuation and aggregation schedulers claim jobs into `PROCESSING`, then publish Kafka job requests; observed publish or delivery-confirmation failures left claimed rows dependent on later stale-job reset instead of immediate durable recovery. | Added shared scheduler dispatch recovery vocabulary, repository-level recovery methods for valuation and aggregation jobs, immediate scheduler recovery hooks for synchronous publish failure and delivery-confirmation timeout, and tests proving first-job failure, mid-batch failure, flush timeout, next-poll recovery, and attempt-budget state transitions. | Keep issue #596 open for PR/CI/QA evidence and Docker-backed scheduler proof. Existing API routes, OpenAPI contracts, database schema, Kafka topics, event payloads, success DTOs, and consumer contracts are preserved; the intentional behavior change is internal `PROCESSING` row recovery on directly observed dispatch failure. | Evidence: [CR-1229-SCHEDULER-DISPATCH-RECOVERY.md](./CR-1229-SCHEDULER-DISPATCH-RECOVERY.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR before commit. |
| CR-1228 | 2026-07-01 | Post-publish bookkeeping repair | Hardened | GitHub issue #595 is valid: direct ingestion routers returned generic `INGESTION_JOB_BOOKKEEPING_FAILED` 500 responses after publish or persist work completed, without explicit publish/work state, retry-safety guidance, supportability reason codes, or a governed repair path. | Added shared bookkeeping recovery vocabulary, expanded failure details with `publish_state`, `work_state`, `published_record_count`, `retry_safe`, `recovery_action`, `recovery_path`, and `supportability_reason_code`, passed published counts from direct ingestion routers, distinguished reference-data persistence from publish, and added protected `POST /ingestion/jobs/{job_id}/bookkeeping/repair` that only repairs jobs with recorded bookkeeping-failure evidence. | Keep issue #595 open for PR/CI/QA evidence and Docker-backed proof against the real database and Kafka-backed ingestion stack; apply the evidence-gated repair pattern to adjacent partial-failure operator workflows through issue-backed slices. Existing route paths, success DTOs, HTTP 500 status, and `INGESTION_JOB_BOOKKEEPING_FAILED` code are preserved; the protected repair endpoint is additive. | Evidence: [CR-1228-POST-PUBLISH-BOOKKEEPING-REPAIR.md](./CR-1228-POST-PUBLISH-BOOKKEEPING-REPAIR.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR before commit. |
| CR-1227 | 2026-07-01 | Ingestion retry recovery responses | Hardened | GitHub issue #598 is valid: ingestion job retry responses mixed duplicate/unsupported blockers, publish failures, and post-publish bookkeeping failures through local detail bodies or raw exception propagation, leaving operators without one stable recovery outcome/remediation shape. | Added a centralized ingestion-job retry recovery detail builder, routed not-found, unsupported, partial unsupported, blocked, duplicate blocked, publish failed, bookkeeping failed, and audit-write failed paths through stable `outcome`, `remediation`, and `recovery_path` fields, changed retry publish failures to governed HTTP 500 responses after preserving replay-audit and failed-job side effects, and updated OpenAPI plus focused unit/integration coverage. | Keep issue #598 open for PR/CI/QA evidence and Docker-backed retry workflow proof; apply the same outcome/remediation pattern to adjacent recovery endpoints through issue-backed slices. Existing route path, HTTP statuses, success DTO, replay audit persistence, and job failure persistence are preserved; retry publish failure now has an explicit `INGESTION_RETRY_PUBLISH_FAILED` contract. | Evidence: [CR-1227-INGESTION-RETRY-RECOVERY-RESPONSES.md](./CR-1227-INGESTION-RETRY-RECOVERY-RESPONSES.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR before commit. |
| CR-1226 | 2026-07-01 | Readiness dependency isolation | Hardened | GitHub issue #599 is valid: shared `/health/ready` orchestration ran dependency checks through one aggregate `asyncio.gather` without a per-dependency timeout or explicit timeout/error/misconfiguration classification, so one slow, unexpected, or invalidly configured dependency check could dominate the readiness request and collapse operator evidence into generic unavailable posture. | Added per-dependency timeout handling, configuration pre-checks, and failure isolation in `portfolio_common.health`; classified dependency statuses as `ok`, `unavailable`, `timeout`, `misconfigured`, or `error`; preserved the existing top-level ready/not-ready response shape; and added focused health tests for DB timeout, Kafka timeout, Kafka misconfiguration, exception isolation, cached ready, cached not-ready, unknown dependencies, and mixed states. | Keep issue #599 open for PR/CI/QA evidence and Docker-backed service readiness proof; extend the same bounded-dependency pattern if future readiness checks add dependency types beyond database and Kafka. No route path, database schema, Kafka topic, or runtime topology changed. | Evidence: [CR-1226-READINESS-DEPENDENCY-ISOLATION.md](./CR-1226-READINESS-DEPENDENCY-ISOLATION.md); focused health tests passed with 10 tests; scoped Ruff lint passed. |
| CR-1225 | 2026-07-01 | Ingestion rate-limit scope guard | Hardened | GitHub issue #684 remained open after CR-1196: runtime scope was explicit, but no deterministic guard prevented docs or config-contract truth from drifting back into global enforcement claims while the default limiter still used local-process state. | Added `scripts/ingestion_rate_limit_scope_guard.py`, `make ingestion-rate-limit-scope-guard`, lint-lane wiring, pass/fail guard tests, and docs/context updates. | CR-1224 | 2026-07-01 | Outbox recovery outcome metrics | Hardened | GitHub issue #670 still had an observability gap after CR-1223: recovery attempts were auditable and listable, but operators had no bounded fleet-level metric for accepted, rejected, missing-row, or unexpected-error recovery outcomes. | Added `outbox_recovery_attempts_total` with bounded `recovery_action`, `outcome`, and stable `reason` labels; instrumented the governed requeue service path for success/rejected/not-found/error outcomes; extended monitoring-label tests to reject sensitive/high-cardinality labels such as `outbox_id`, `correlation_id`, and `requested_by`. | Keep issue #670 open for Docker-backed integrated proof that dispatcher-created failed rows can be requeued through QCP and processed by the dispatcher, plus alert-threshold decisions after runtime usage. | Evidence: [CR-1224-OUTBOX-RECOVERY-OUTCOME-METRICS.md](./CR-1224-OUTBOX-RECOVERY-OUTCOME-METRICS.md); validation evidence is recorded in the CR before commit. |

| CR-1192 | 2026-06-30 | Metrics access policy | Hardened | GitHub issue #678 is valid: the shared HTTP bootstrap exposed `/metrics` without an explicit app-level access policy, trust-boundary contract, or tests for protected scrape behavior; implementation review later found worker-runtime metrics exposure and direct bootstrap env reads still bypassed the intended platform pattern. | Added a shared metrics access policy with default `internal_open` private-scrape compatibility and optional bearer-token protection through `LOTUS_METRICS_ACCESS_TOKEN`; moved metrics-token env parsing into approved common settings; enforced the policy centrally in the standard HTTP bootstrap and web-backed worker runtime, including pre-existing worker metrics routes; documented `/metrics` as an operational scrape endpoint in OpenAPI and observability docs; added tests for default, protected-deny, protected-allow, OpenAPI policy documentation, worker-runtime policy enforcement, and config guard coverage. | Issue #678 remains open for PR/CI/QA evidence plus platform/ingress validation that public ingress does not route `/metrics` without an explicit operational policy. No business API route, database schema, metric name, metric label, default metrics success behavior, or success payload shape changed; the intentional behavior change is opt-in denial when a metrics token is configured. No wiki update required because no repo-local wiki runbook currently describes metrics ingress configuration. | Evidence: [CR-1192-METRICS-ACCESS-POLICY.md](./CR-1192-METRICS-ACCESS-POLICY.md); focused shared-bootstrap, worker-runtime, and config-guard tests passed with 19 tests; config access guard passed; scoped Ruff lint/format and `git diff --check` passed. |
| CR-1191 | 2026-06-30 | Query-control-plane problem details | Hardened | GitHub issue #677 is valid: representative query-control-plane routers exposed errors as bare `{"detail": ...}` responses and sometimes mapped application exceptions with raw `str(exc)`, leaving downstream clients without stable error codes, correlation context, safe metadata, or truthful problem-details media-type docs. | Added a shared query-control-plane problem-details model, exception, response example helper, and app-level handler; migrated representative core-snapshot, analytics-input, simulation, operations-support, and portfolio source-evidence failure mappings to top-level problem-details payloads with stable QCP error codes, correlation IDs, bounded details, and source-safe metadata; updated affected OpenAPI examples and tests; tightened the response helper so migrated examples use `application/problem+json` while unmigrated bare-detail examples stay explicit `application/json` with a legacy schema. | Issue #677 remains open for remaining route-family migration, typed simulation service exceptions, a future deterministic guard once the migration baseline is broad enough, and PR/CI/QA evidence. HTTP status codes are preserved for migrated paths; the intentional behavior change is replacing raw bare-detail error bodies with problem-details fields on migrated routes and making OpenAPI media types truthful per route. Operations support and source-evidence errors now expose field/resource/source-product context through bounded metadata rather than raw service exception text. No wiki update required because no operator-facing command or wiki runbook changed; repo wiki publication check still reports unrelated existing published-wiki drift. | Evidence: [CR-1191-QUERY-CONTROL-PLANE-PROBLEM-DETAILS.md](./CR-1191-QUERY-CONTROL-PLANE-PROBLEM-DETAILS.md); focused query-control-plane unit/router/OpenAPI tests passed with 78 unit tests, 67 integration dependency tests, 40 control-plane app contract tests, 46 operations route-family tests, 10 source-evidence proof tests, and 101 affected integration-router route-family tests; scoped Ruff lint/format, OpenAPI, API vocabulary, Spectral blocker subset, typecheck, wiki-docs, no-alias, and `git diff --check` passed. |
| CR-1190 | 2026-06-30 | Event runtime DLQ topic guard | Hardened | GitHub issue #671 is valid: RFC-0083 runtime event contract governance validated direct `publish_message(topic=...)` calls but missed `BaseConsumer` DLQ publication because the runtime publish uses `self.dlq_topic`. | Extended the guard to discover `BaseConsumer` subclasses, resolve `dlq_topic=` constructor wiring from literals, config constants, and local aliases, fail unresolved dynamic or uncataloged DLQ topics, and cataloged `dlq.persistence_service` as a governed direct Kafka DLQ topic with BaseConsumer payload, replay consumers, header, and evidence expectations. | Issue #671 remains open for PR/CI/QA evidence. No API route, DTO, Kafka topic name, database schema, DLQ payload field, consumer group, or runtime publish behavior changed; the intentional behavior change is CI/governance failure for miswired DLQ topics. No wiki update required because no operator-facing command or wiki runbook changed. | Evidence: [CR-1190-EVENT-RUNTIME-DLQ-TOPIC-GUARD.md](./CR-1190-EVENT-RUNTIME-DLQ-TOPIC-GUARD.md); focused event runtime contract guard tests passed with 14 tests; standalone event runtime contract guard passed; scoped Ruff lint/format and `git diff --check` passed. |
| CR-1189 | 2026-06-30 | Source-batch fingerprint semantics | Hardened | GitHub issue #676 is valid: selected source-data products populated `source_batch_fingerprint` with request-scope or snapshot fingerprints, conflating response identity with upstream source-batch lineage. | Changed ClientTaxProfile, ClientRestrictionProfile, SustainabilityPreferenceProfile, and DpmPortfolioUniverseCandidate responses to leave `source_batch_fingerprint` null when true upstream source-batch evidence is unavailable, while preserving existing `snapshot_id` and DPM `page.request_scope_fingerprint`; updated DTO and RFC-0083 catalog wording. | Issue #676 remains open for broader source-data product audit, true persisted source-batch wiring, PR/CI/QA evidence, and conformance guard coverage. | CR-1188 | 2026-06-30 | Ingestion idempotency full fingerprint | Hardened | GitHub issue #675 is valid: idempotency conflict checks compared source-safe/redacted payload fingerprints, so same endpoint/key reuse with different sensitive values could replay the previous job because redaction collapsed those values before hashing. | Added nullable `ingestion_jobs.request_payload_fingerprint` and an idempotency lookup index, persisted full canonical non-reversible request fingerprints before redaction, kept durable request payload evidence redacted, and changed conflict detection to prefer the full fingerprint while retaining legacy fallback for historical rows. | Issue #675 remains open for PR/CI/QA evidence and broader #559 payload retention/encryption/replay policy follow-up. No route path, response DTO, Kafka topic, request DTO, durable redacted payload shape, or same-key/same-payload replay behavior changed; same-key/different-sensitive-payload reuse now intentionally conflicts. | CR-1186 | 2026-06-30 | Outbox retry eligibility | Hardened | GitHub issue #669 is valid: retryable outbox publish failures incremented `retry_count` and updated `last_attempted_at`, but remained immediately eligible on every dispatcher poll because the claim query only filtered `status = 'PENDING'`. | Added nullable `outbox_events.next_attempt_at`, a `status, next_attempt_at, created_at` claim index, runtime retry-delay settings, bounded exponential scheduling, claim filtering by matured eligibility, and tests for retry settings, delay bounds, persisted retry eligibility, and skipping immature rows. | Issue #669 remains open for PR/CI/QA evidence, Docker-backed integration proof, pending-waiting metrics, and any shared max-elapsed retry budget. No wiki update required because no operator-facing command or published runbook changed. | Evidence: [CR-1186-OUTBOX-RETRY-ELIGIBILITY.md](./CR-1186-OUTBOX-RETRY-ELIGIBILITY.md); unit outbox dispatcher tests passed with 7 tests; scoped Ruff lint/format, Alembic single-head check, and `git diff --check` passed; focused DB-backed outbox integration tests were attempted but blocked before test bodies by unavailable Docker engine. |

| CR-877 | 2026-06-02 | Deptry dependency-usage baseline scoping | Measured | The report-only workflow ran `python -m deptry .`, which could scan local `.venv` artifacts and generated build output. A truthful production-source scan reports 928 `DEP003` findings across 485 files, dominated by first-party module modeling and undeclared runtime dependency metadata. | Scoped the workflow dependency baseline to `python -m deptry src --extend-exclude "src/services/query_service/build"` and recorded the measured baseline in the scorecard and baseline report. | Make root runtime dependency metadata and first-party package modeling truthful, then ratchet deptry into an enforceable source gate. | Evidence: [CR-877-DEPTRY-DEPENDENCY-BASELINE-SCOPING.md](./CR-877-DEPTRY-DEPENDENCY-BASELINE-SCOPING.md); local deptry source baseline => 928 findings. |
| CR-001 | 2026-03-10 | Async orchestration, heavy-gate runtime, quiescence barriers | Hardened | Post-RFC-081 heavy validation exposed real orchestration defects: duplicate position-timeseries fan-out, incorrect same-day aggregation re-arm semantics, quiescence false positives, and stale runtime assumptions in test-stack orchestration. | Hardened timeseries orchestration, added quiescence-aware cleanup with quiet-window support, removed false-positive blockers from quiescence, tightened E2E setup to wait for business-complete states, and refreshed failure-recovery evidence. | Keep watching CI/main heavy gates. If hosted CI shows environment-specific regressions, treat them as extensions of this scope until stabilized. | Commits `96a17a9`, `a5e4288`; local validation: `pytest tests/e2e -q -x -s` => `56 passed`; `python scripts/failure_recovery_gate.py --build --enforce` passed; RFC 081 reopened for hardening. |
| CR-002 | 2026-03-10 | Portfolio aggregation eligibility query shape | Hardened | `find_and_claim_eligible_jobs()` in portfolio aggregation/timeseries repositories allowed a correlated subquery to degrade into an accidental `FROM daily_position_snapshots, portfolio_aggregation_jobs` pattern. This prevented first eligible portfolio-day jobs from being claimed on a fresh workflow. | Rewrote the prior-day and completeness gates to use explicit `select(1)` correlation and correlated snapshot subqueries. Added unit query-shape tests and DB-backed integration coverage for first-day, mixed-epoch, and prior-day eligibility behavior. | Converge duplicate repository implementations so the same SQL is not maintained in two places. | Commits within `96a17a9`; tests: `tests/unit/services/timeseries_generator_service/.../test_unit_timeseries_repo.py`, `tests/unit/services/portfolio_aggregation_service/repositories/test_timeseries_repository.py`, `tests/integration/services/timeseries_generator_service/test_timeseries_repository_integration.py`; `tests/e2e/test_5_day_workflow.py` => `6 passed`. |
| CR-003 | 2026-03-10 | Cashflow replay correctness and epoch-versioned persistence | Hardened | Back-dated replay and stale-event flows could fail because cashflows were not safely versioned across epochs. Lower-level contract coverage was insufficient for replay-oriented persistence behavior. | Added epoch-versioned cashflow persistence support, updated cashflow consumer/runtime behavior, added integration tests for query-side cashflow repository behavior, and added unit tests for the consumer/manager paths. | Review remaining replay-sensitive repositories for the same epoch-versioning assumptions. | Commit `8a32e00`; migration `d1e2f3a4b5c6_feat_allow_epoch_versioned_cashflows.py`; query integration and cashflow consumer unit tests. |
| CR-004 | 2026-03-10 | Valuation scheduling for current-day positions | Hardened | Current-day market prices were not always creating the required valuation work deterministically. This left heavy workflows dependent on indirect or lagging triggers. | Updated valuation repositories and price-event trigger logic, then later removed the dead duplicate trigger surface from `position_valuation_calculator` once ownership was finalized in CR-091. | Keep valuation trigger ownership in `valuation_orchestrator_service`; do not reintroduce calculator-local scheduling paths. | Commit `f262393`; enduring evidence: `tests/unit/libs/portfolio-common/test_valuation_job_repository.py`; live ownership finalized in [CR-091-POSITION-VALUATION-WORKER-OWNERSHIP-CLEANUP.md](./CR-091-POSITION-VALUATION-WORKER-OWNERSHIP-CLEANUP.md). |
| CR-005 | 2026-03-10 | Test-stack environment, ports, and module isolation | Hardened | The previous Docker-backed test harness mixed fixed-port assumptions, shared compose project names, and weak cleanup semantics. This reduced confidence and created false negatives. | Introduced dynamic runtime allocation, project-scoped compose usage, Kafka readiness, quiescence-based cleanup, and corrected E2E setup/wait behavior. | Consider splitting full-stack E2E from smaller Docker-backed integration profiles to reduce runtime cost further. | Tests under `tests/test_support/`; `tests/conftest.py`; local `e2e-all`, `integration-all`, and failure-recovery gate all green. |
| CR-006 | 2026-03-10 | Duplicate repository and service logic across split services | Hardened | Review confirmed the real structural duplication was in repository pairs, not in consumer managers. `ValuationRepository` was duplicated in full between `valuation_orchestrator_service` and `position_valuation_calculator`. `TimeseriesRepository` was near-duplicated between `portfolio_aggregation_service` and `timeseries_generator_service`, with only a worker-local `get_position_timeseries(...)` difference. | Extracted shared valuation query/claim logic into `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/valuation_repository_base.py` and shared timeseries query/claim logic into `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/timeseries_repository_base.py`. Reduced all four service-local repository files to thin wrappers that preserve import paths, service-local metric hooks, and the worker-only `get_position_timeseries(...)` helper. Validated both converged paths with unit and integration repository suites. | Watch for future duplication in runtime scaffolding, but repository-body convergence for the reviewed valuation/timeseries pairs is complete. Do not force consumer-manager unification unless later review shows reusable scaffolding with a real payoff. | Evidence: [CR-006-DUPLICATE-REPOSITORY-REVIEW.md](./CR-006-DUPLICATE-REPOSITORY-REVIEW.md); `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/valuation_repository_base.py`; `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/timeseries_repository_base.py`; service-local repository wrappers; valuation and timeseries repository unit/integration tests. |
| CR-007 | 2026-03-10 | Failure-recovery gate semantics | Hardened | Review confirmed the gate is evaluating bounded recovery, not strict full drain. A run can pass while `drain_seconds_to_baseline = timeout` if backlog age and DLQ/replay pressure recover within allowed bounds. That behavior is coherent, but it was previously ambiguous in the gate output. | Added explicit recovery classification to the gate result: `FULLY_DRAINED`, `BOUNDED_RECOVERY`, `FAILED_RECOVERY`. Added direct unit tests for the gate decision matrix and updated the review note accordingly. | If policy tuning is desired later, revisit thresholds separately. The semantic ambiguity is now closed. | Evidence: [CR-007-FAILURE-RECOVERY-REVIEW.md](./CR-007-FAILURE-RECOVERY-REVIEW.md); `tests/unit/scripts/test_failure_recovery_gate.py` => `12 passed`; `ruff check` passed on gate + tests; report `output/task-runs/20260310T050658Z-failure-recovery-gate.md`. |
| CR-008 | 2026-03-10 | Control-plane job health query shape | Hardened | The issue in `query_service` support/operations endpoints was query fan-out, not missing worker indexes. One overview/SLO request executed many separate aggregates over the same valuation and aggregation job tables. Existing indexed columns (`portfolio_id`, `status`, business-date columns) are broadly adequate for the current control-plane predicates. | Consolidated valuation and aggregation job health into combined summary queries and updated `OperationsService` to consume those summaries. Added unit coverage for the summary-query repository methods and the updated service interface. | If hosted runtime metrics later show true DB pressure on worker claim paths, review those separately under a scheduler/backlog performance batch. Do not add speculative indexes before evidence exists. | Evidence: [CR-008-CONTROL-PLANE-JOB-HEALTH-QUERY-REVIEW.md](./CR-008-CONTROL-PLANE-JOB-HEALTH-QUERY-REVIEW.md); `src/services/query_service/app/repositories/operations_repository.py`; `src/services/query_service/app/services/operations_service.py`; unit tests on repository + service summary behavior. |
| CR-009 | 2026-03-10 | Query vs query-control-plane source ownership | Hardened | RFC 81 split the runtime/API surface correctly, but control-plane source ownership was still blurred: `query_control_plane_service` imported routers and enterprise-readiness logic directly from `query_service`, and control-plane tests were split across `query_service` paths. | Moved control-plane router modules into `src/services/query_control_plane_service/app/routers/`, moved router dependency tests into `tests/integration/services/query_control_plane_service/`, moved router unit tests into `tests/unit/services/query_control_plane_service/routers/`, added service-local enterprise-readiness middleware for the control-plane app, updated `query_control_plane_service/app/main.py` to import only local app modules for that API surface, and removed the stale control-plane router copies from `src/services/query_service/app/routers/`. | Shared DTO/service/query logic may still remain in `query_service` where that is the canonical business/query layer. If later review shows a reusable shared query package is warranted, handle that as a separate convergence batch rather than re-blurring control-plane app ownership. | Evidence: [CR-009-QUERY-CONTROL-PLANE-OWNERSHIP-REVIEW.md](./CR-009-QUERY-CONTROL-PLANE-OWNERSHIP-REVIEW.md); local control-plane router package; moved control-plane unit/integration router tests; `tests/integration/services/query_control_plane_service/*` app-contract + dependency suites; `tests/unit/services/query_control_plane_service/routers/*`. |
| CR-010 | 2026-03-10 | Worker claim-path scheduler and backlog semantics | Hardened | Worker claim paths already use sound locking/claim semantics (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` with atomic claim updates) for valuation, aggregation, and reprocessing jobs. The main residual structural risk is duplicated claim repository code, already tracked in `CR-006`. One concrete inconsistency did exist: aggregation stale-job resets did not refresh `updated_at` while valuation stale-job resets did. | Reviewed worker claim implementations and stale-reset paths. Hardened both aggregation repository copies so stale resets now set `updated_at = now()` in addition to resetting status. Added unit coverage for the reset statement shape. | Converge duplicated valuation and aggregation repositories under `CR-006` so future claim-path fixes land once. Consider consistent reset metrics across worker types later. | Evidence: [CR-010-WORKER-CLAIM-PATH-REVIEW.md](./CR-010-WORKER-CLAIM-PATH-REVIEW.md); `src/services/timeseries_generator_service/app/repositories/timeseries_repository.py`; `src/services/portfolio_aggregation_service/app/repositories/timeseries_repository.py`; unit repository tests for stale reset. |

inancial-calculator-engine is not dead, but only its core/ and logic/ layers are truly shared. The ngine/ layer (TransactionProcessor + recalculation metrics) was only used by cost_calculator_service, so it was service-owned orchestration disguised as shared engine code. | Moved TransactionProcessor and recalculation monitoring into cost_calculator_service, moved the direct transaction-processor unit test into the service test area, updated manifest/doc references, and removed the dead 
inancial-calculator-engine/src/engine/ package. | If the remaining shared library is renamed later, do it as a dedicated vocabulary/package-governance slice. The current ownership fix is sufficient without broader package churn. | Evidence: [CR-013-FINANCIAL-CALCULATOR-ENGINE-SCOPE-REVIEW.md](./CR-013-FINANCIAL-CALCULATOR-ENGINE-SCOPE-REVIEW.md); src/services/calculators/cost_calculator_service/app/transaction_processor.py; src/services/calculators/cost_calculator_service/app/monitoring.py; moved transaction-processor unit tests. |
| CR-014 | 2026-03-10 | Startup/runtime contracts for dependency-critical services | Hardened | The hot-path startup review found that `ingestion_service` tolerated Kafka producer initialization failure by logging a fatal error and continuing startup with `app_state[\"kafka_producer\"] = None`. That let a write-ingress service advertise readiness while unable to fulfill its core publish contract. | Hardened `ingestion_service` to fail fast on Kafka producer initialization failure and added lifecycle-level integration coverage asserting startup raises when Kafka initialization fails. | Continue the same review pattern across other dependency-critical services; only treat dependency failure as degraded if the service contract is truly still valid. | Evidence: [CR-014-STARTUP-RUNTIME-CONTRACT-REVIEW.md](./CR-014-STARTUP-RUNTIME-CONTRACT-REVIEW.md); `src/services/ingestion_service/app/main.py`; `tests/integration/services/ingestion_service/test_ingestion_main_app_contract.py`. |

The duplication had already drifted into inconsistent fatal-error semantics: some workers re-raised `ConsumerManager` failures, others logged critical errors and exited without re-raising. | Extracted the shared runtime contract into `portfolio_common.worker_runtime.run_instrumented_worker_service(...)`, migrated the web-backed worker services onto it, and standardized fatal worker runtime failures to log and re-raise consistently. Added direct unit tests for the shared helper. | Review remaining non-web worker mains separately. Do not force `persistence_service` or pure HTTP services into this helper unless their contracts genuinely converge. | Evidence: [CR-015-WEB-WORKER-STARTUP-CONTRACT-REVIEW.md](./CR-015-WEB-WORKER-STARTUP-CONTRACT-REVIEW.md); `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/worker_runtime.py`; updated worker `main.py` files; `tests/unit/libs/portfolio-common/test_worker_runtime.py`. |

Do not turn the helper into a generic app factory unless startup contracts truly converge. | Evidence: [CR-016-PURE-HTTP-BOOTSTRAP-REVIEW.md](./CR-016-PURE-HTTP-BOOTSTRAP-REVIEW.md); `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/http_app_bootstrap.py`; app mains for query, query-control-plane, event-replay, financial-reconciliation, and ingestion; `pytest` app-contract suites for query, query-control-plane, event-replay, and ingestion => `25 passed`. |

| CR-021 | 2026-03-10 | Docker image preflight and local runtime drift | Hardened | Docker-backed heavy tests still assumed required base images were already present locally, which caused late compose failures when `cp-zookeeper` / `cp-kafka` images were missing. `docker-compose.yml` also still carried a stale `financial-calculator-engine` volume mount after CR-020 removed that package. | Added Docker image preflight/pull behavior to `tests/test_support/docker_stack.py`, added unit coverage for missing-image and failed-pull cases, and removed the stale `financial-calculator-engine` volume mount from `cost_calculator_service` in `docker-compose.yml`. | If image sourcing becomes a recurring problem, define explicit mirror/cache warm-up policy in dev/CI bootstrap. The concrete local runtime drift reviewed here is now closed. | Evidence: [CR-021-DOCKER-IMAGE-PREFLIGHT-REVIEW.md](./CR-021-DOCKER-IMAGE-PREFLIGHT-REVIEW.md); `tests/test_support/docker_stack.py`; `tests/unit/test_support/test_docker_stack.py`; `docker-compose.yml`. |
| CR-022 | 2026-03-10 | Unit-test async cleanup warning debt | Hardened | Branch-scoped unit premerge runs still emitted `aclose()`-not-awaited warnings from test doubles around `get_async_db_session`, plus one Kafka payload-format test that exercised DB recording even though DB persistence was not the behavior under test. | Replaced raw async-generator monkeypatches with explicit single-yield async iterators in the affected tests, mocked `_record_consumer_dlq_event` in the Kafka payload-format test, and re-ran the warning-producing tests with warnings enabled to prove the warnings were gone. | Keep future async cleanup warnings classified as test-harness debt first unless a targeted reproduction proves a production resource-lifecycle bug. | Evidence: [CR-022-UNIT-TEST-ASYNC-CLEANUP-WARNING-REVIEW.md](./CR-022-UNIT-TEST-ASYNC-CLEANUP-WARNING-REVIEW.md); warning-producing unit tests rerun clean with `-W default`. |
| CR-023 | 2026-03-10 | Unit Kafka producer noise and stale legacy test leftovers | Hardened | The unit manifest still printed noisy librdkafka connection failures because the unit tier allowed accidental construction of the real `confluent_kafka.Producer`. The repo also still had three stale legacy analytics test directories that only contained `__pycache__` leftovers. | Added a unit-tier autouse fixture that monkeypatches `portfolio_common.kafka_utils.Producer` to a `MagicMock` and resets the producer singleton before/after each unit test, added explicit producer shutdown support in `portfolio_common.kafka_utils`, re-ran the full unit manifest to prove the suite is quiet, and removed the stale legacy test directories. | If future tests need a real Kafka client, move them to integration coverage instead of weakening the unit tier. | Evidence: [CR-023-UNIT-KAFKA-PRODUCER-NOISE-REVIEW.md](./CR-023-UNIT-KAFKA-PRODUCER-NOISE-REVIEW.md); `tests/unit/conftest.py`; `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/kafka_utils.py`; `python scripts/test_manifest.py --suite unit --quiet` => `893 passed, 6 deselected` with no producer-noise stderr. |

| CR-065 | 2026-03-10 | Ingestion job shared schema Swagger depth | Hardened | The event-replay and ingestion-operations router surfaces were already operationally strong, but the shared ingestion-job DTO layer still had thin list-wrapper examples, thinner backlog summary metadata, and no explicit shared policy flag for replay dry-run support. | Added example-backed list-wrapper schema depth for ingestion jobs, job failures, and reprocessing queue health; added explicit shared operational metadata for the oldest backlog job and replay dry-run support; and added event-replay app OpenAPI assertions to lock the richer shared-schema behavior in place. | Review only the remaining operational DTOs if they still fall materially below the now-hardened control-plane standard. | Evidence: [CR-065-INGESTION-JOB-SHARED-SCHEMA-SWAGGER-REVIEW.md](./CR-065-INGESTION-JOB-SHARED-SCHEMA-SWAGGER-REVIEW.md); `src/services/ingestion_service/app/DTOs/ingestion_job_dto.py`; `tests/integration/services/event_replay_service/test_event_replay_app.py`; OpenAPI gate and vocabulary inventory still pass. |
| CR-066 | 2026-03-10 | Write-plane acknowledgement schema Swagger depth | Hardened | After the larger write-plane DTO families were strengthened, the smaller shared acknowledgement and replay-request DTOs were still acceptable but used older, thinner operational wording. | Tightened the shared acknowledgement and replay-request descriptions in `ingestion_ack_dto.py` and `reprocessing_dto.py`, then added an ingestion app OpenAPI assertion to lock the stronger wording in place. | Remaining Swagger work should now focus only on surfaces that still show materially weaker schema depth than the current baseline. | Evidence: [CR-066-WRITE-PLANE-ACKNOWLEDGEMENT-SCHEMA-SWAGGER-REVIEW.md](./CR-066-WRITE-PLANE-ACKNOWLEDGEMENT-SCHEMA-SWAGGER-REVIEW.md); `src/services/ingestion_service/app/DTOs/ingestion_ack_dto.py`; `src/services/ingestion_service/app/DTOs/reprocessing_dto.py`; `tests/integration/services/ingestion_service/test_ingestion_main_app_contract.py`; OpenAPI gate and vocabulary inventory still pass. |

If a future external caller genuinely needs a compatibility alias, add it only with explicit justification. | Evidence: [CR-068-TEST-MANIFEST-LEGACY-ALIAS-REVIEW.md](./CR-068-TEST-MANIFEST-LEGACY-ALIAS-REVIEW.md); `scripts/test_manifest.py`; `tests/unit/services/query_service/test_test_manifest.py`. |
| CR-069 | 2026-03-10 | Canonical contract naming cleanup | Hardened | Active RFCs, conformance reports, and the `Makefile` still referenced deleted suite aliases (`buy-rfc`, `sell-rfc`, `fx-rfc`, etc.) even after the manifest had been canonicalized. That left current-state documentation and entrypoints out of sync with live CI and the actual test manifest. | Removed the legacy alias targets from `Makefile`, updated active RFC/conformance docs to the canonical `transaction-*-contract` names, and regenerated the API vocabulary artifact so current-state standards reflect the live OpenAPI surface. | Historical review records may still mention removed aliases intentionally as migration evidence. Keep only current-state docs/tooling alias-free. | Evidence: [CR-069-CANONICAL-CONTRACT-NAMING-CLEANUP.md](./CR-069-CANONICAL-CONTRACT-NAMING-CLEANUP.md); `Makefile`; active RFC/conformance docs; `docs/standards/api-vocabulary/lotus-core-api-vocabulary.v1.json`. |
| CR-070 | 2026-03-10 | JSON error response example enforcement | Hardened | The remaining Swagger debt was systemic: many JSON error responses, especially FastAPI-generated `422` validation errors, still had no examples even though the router and DTO passes had already improved success-path documentation. | Extended the shared OpenAPI enrichment layer to generate non-2xx JSON response examples, tightened the OpenAPI quality gate to enforce error-response examples, and added unit coverage for both the enrichment and the gate. | New HTTP apps must keep using the shared enrichment path or they will regress immediately under the strengthened gate. | Evidence: [CR-070-ERROR-RESPONSE-EXAMPLE-GATE-REVIEW.md](./CR-070-ERROR-RESPONSE-EXAMPLE-GATE-REVIEW.md); `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/openapi_enrichment.py`; `scripts/openapi_quality_gate.py`; `tests/unit/libs/portfolio-common/test_openapi_enrichment.py`; `tests/unit/services/query_service/test_openapi_quality_gate.py`. |
| CR-071 | 2026-03-10 | Parameter description enforcement | Hardened | After the router and DTO passes, a small but real Swagger usability gap remained: some query/path parameters still had examples but no descriptions, concentrated in `query_service` lookups and `event_replay_service` operations endpoints. | Added the missing parameter descriptions/examples on the remaining weak endpoints and tightened `scripts/openapi_quality_gate.py` so parameter descriptions are mandatory for declared parameters. | New endpoints must document every parameter on day one or they will fail the strengthened gate. | Evidence: [CR-071-PARAMETER-DESCRIPTION-GATE-REVIEW.md](./CR-071-PARAMETER-DESCRIPTION-GATE-REVIEW.md); `src/services/query_service/app/routers/lookups.py`; `src/services/event_replay_service/app/routers/ingestion_operations.py`; `scripts/openapi_quality_gate.py`; `tests/unit/services/query_service/test_openapi_quality_gate.py`. |
| CR-072 | 2026-03-10 | Empty transaction batch contract alignment | Hardened | `POST /ingest/transactions` rejected `{"transactions": []}` with `422` because `TransactionIngestionRequest.transactions` still had `min_length=1`, even though the publish path already handled an empty list safely and the E2E/API expectation treats an empty batch as a valid no-op submission. | Removed the `min_length=1` constraint, updated the transaction ingestion schema description/examples to make the no-op semantics explicit, added direct ingestion router coverage, and aligned the OpenAPI contract test with the new example set. | None for the empty-batch path; the behavior is now covered below E2E and matches the public contract. | Evidence: [CR-072-EMPTY-TRANSACTION-BATCH-CONTRACT-REVIEW.md](./CR-072-EMPTY-TRANSACTION-BATCH-CONTRACT-REVIEW.md); `src/services/ingestion_service/app/DTOs/transaction_dto.py`; `tests/integration/services/ingestion_service/test_ingestion_routers.py`; `tests/integration/services/ingestion_service/test_ingestion_main_app_contract.py`; `tests/e2e/test_ingestion_service_api.py`. |
| CR-073 | 2026-03-11 | Control-plane documentation ownership drift | Hardened | Active RFCs and ADRs still referenced deleted `query_service` control-plane router paths even after RFC 81 moved that surface into `query_control_plane_service`. This left current-state architecture docs pointing to the wrong implementation tree. | Rewrote active RFC and ADR references to the live `query_control_plane_service` router paths and corrected the stale source-path header comment in `src/services/query_control_plane_service/app/routers/simulation.py`. Left review-history artifacts unchanged where they intentionally describe pre-refactor state. | Continue scanning for current-state docs that still point at removed runtime files whenever future ownership moves land. Do not rewrite review notes that intentionally document the prior state. | Evidence: [CR-073-CONTROL-PLANE-DOCUMENTATION-OWNERSHIP-DRIFT-REVIEW.md](./CR-073-CONTROL-PLANE-DOCUMENTATION-OWNERSHIP-DRIFT-REVIEW.md); active RFC/ADR path rewrites; `src/services/query_control_plane_service/app/routers/simulation.py`. |
| CR-074 | 2026-03-11 | Grafana service-topology visibility | Hardened | Prometheus already scraped the post-RFC-81 service set, but the bundled Grafana dashboard still exposed only generic DB/outbox counters. Operators had no direct panel for per-service request load or scrape availability across the split service topology. | Added service-scoped HTTP request-rate and service-availability panels to `grafana/dashboards/portfolio_analytics.json`, keyed exactly to the live Prometheus `job_name` set from `prometheus/prometheus.yml`. | Add deeper domain-specific panels later if operators need reconciliation/replay/stage-gate visibility directly in Grafana. The basic topology-visibility gap is now closed. | Evidence: [CR-074-GRAFANA-SERVICE-TOPOLOGY-REVIEW.md](./CR-074-GRAFANA-SERVICE-TOPOLOGY-REVIEW.md); `grafana/dashboards/portfolio_analytics.json`; `prometheus/prometheus.yml`. |
| CR-075 | 2026-03-11 | Test harness output control | Hardened | `tests/conftest.py` emitted detailed lifecycle output unconditionally, including per-service health confirmations and DB cleanup chatter. That made routine Docker-backed validation harder to scan even when the underlying tests were healthy. | Added `tests/test_support/output_control.py`, introduced `LOTUS_TESTS_VERBOSE`, and routed `tests/conftest.py` lifecycle output through the helper so only the highest-value milestones print by default while detailed noise becomes opt-in. Added direct unit coverage for the helper and reran representative integration/E2E slices. | Keep new harness output behind the same explicit verbosity switch. If future fixtures add lifecycle chatter, route them through the shared helper rather than printing directly. | Evidence: [CR-075-TEST-HARNESS-OUTPUT-CONTROL-REVIEW.md](./CR-075-TEST-HARNESS-OUTPUT-CONTROL-REVIEW.md); `tests/test_support/output_control.py`; `tests/conftest.py`; `tests/unit/test_support/test_output_control.py`; `tests/integration/services/ingestion_service/test_ingestion_main_app_contract.py`; `tests/e2e/test_ingestion_service_api.py`. |
| CR-076 | 2026-03-11 | E2E failure-scenario output noise | Hardened | `tests/e2e/test_failure_scenarios.py` still emitted verbose recovery-step chatter directly to stdout, which made a successful outage-recovery run noisy even after CR-075 quieted the shared harness. | Routed failure-scenario progress output through `tests.test_support.output_control.emit_test_output`, keeping major recovery milestones visible while moving repetitive readiness/DLQ chatter behind verbose mode. | Apply the same pattern to any future high-noise E2E modules instead of adding direct `print(...)` calls. | Evidence: [CR-076-E2E-FAILURE-SCENARIO-OUTPUT-NOISE-REVIEW.md](./CR-076-E2E-FAILURE-SCENARIO-OUTPUT-NOISE-REVIEW.md); `tests/e2e/test_failure_scenarios.py`; `tests/test_support/output_control.py`; `tests/conftest.py`; `python -m pytest tests/e2e/test_failure_scenarios.py -q`. |
| CR-077 | 2026-03-11 | DLQ replayer test cleanup noise | Hardened | `tests/integration/tools/test_dlq_replayer.py` still printed Kafka topic-deletion cleanup failures directly to stdout during teardown. That was low-frequency but inconsistent with the broader effort to keep healthy test runs quiet. | Replaced the direct cleanup print with `warnings.warn(...)`, keeping teardown anomalies visible without polluting normal test output. | Follow the same rule for future teardown-only diagnostics: warnings, not unconditional prints. | Evidence: [CR-077-DLQ-REPLAYER-TEST-CLEANUP-NOISE-REVIEW.md](./CR-077-DLQ-REPLAYER-TEST-CLEANUP-NOISE-REVIEW.md); `tests/integration/tools/test_dlq_replayer.py`; `python -m pytest tests/integration/tools/test_dlq_replayer.py -q`. |
| CR-078 | 2026-03-11 | Financial reconciliation observability | Hardened | `financial_reconciliation_service` had no business-level Prometheus telemetry for run outcomes, findings volume, or run duration, even though it is now a first-class control-plane service. That left Grafana blind to the actual reconciliation workload. | Added reconciliation run/finding/duration metrics in `portfolio_common.monitoring`, instrumented the reconciliation service at run finalization, added unit coverage for the metrics hook, and extended the bundled Grafana dashboard with reconciliation outcome and finding panels. | Add queue-depth or backlog gauges later only if the service gains a worker-claim/backlog model. Keep the current instrumentation focused on completed-run behavior. | Evidence: [CR-078-FINANCIAL-RECONCILIATION-OBSERVABILITY-REVIEW.md](./CR-078-FINANCIAL-RECONCILIATION-OBSERVABILITY-REVIEW.md); `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/monitoring.py`; `src/services/financial_reconciliation_service/app/services/reconciliation_service.py`; `tests/unit/services/financial_reconciliation_service/test_reconciliation_service.py`; `grafana/dashboards/portfolio_analytics.json`. |
| CR-079 | 2026-03-11 | Replay observability dashboard coverage | Hardened | Replay telemetry already existed in the shared monitoring layer and was being incremented by `ingestion_job_service`, but the bundled Grafana dashboard did not surface it. That left replay pressure invisible unless operators started from APIs or raw Prometheus queries. | Added Grafana panels for replay audit outcomes and replay duplicate/failure pressure using the existing replay metrics. | If operators later want a single ingestion recovery view, compose replay, backlog, and DLQ signals together in a dedicated dashboard slice rather than overloading the generic overview further. | Evidence: [CR-079-REPLAY-OBSERVABILITY-DASHBOARD-REVIEW.md](./CR-079-REPLAY-OBSERVABILITY-DASHBOARD-REVIEW.md); `grafana/dashboards/portfolio_analytics.json`; `src/libs/portfolio-common/portfolio_common/monitoring.py`; `src/services/ingestion_service/app/services/ingestion_job_service.py`. |
| CR-080 | 2026-03-11 | Ingestion backlog and reprocessing dashboard coverage | Hardened | Ingestion backlog age and reprocessing worker pressure were already exposed through Prometheus, but the bundled Grafana dashboard did not show them.
